agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant with supervisor-only reassignment. The product team requests an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be divided across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Fulfillment / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times from assignment through dispatch."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for physically transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Does not yet exist in the domain model."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The act of binding exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once made, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the only authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a locked Shipment."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed feature to automatically decompose an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each with a different Carrier, bypassing supervisor approval."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment leaves the facility; the system guarantees a 1:1 Carrier assignment at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a core state-integrity constraint. If a Shipment has zero or multiple Carriers, accountability, tracking, and liability are undefined. The system is in a corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment record in any non-terminal state, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization invariant protecting chain-of-custody integrity. Allowing unauthorized reassignment breaks auditability and accountability.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment operation that lacks a Supervisor credential must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment has exactly one Carrier (dispatch-time guarantee).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — a shipment leaves on one truck with one carrier. Violating this means the shipment cannot be physically dispatched.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation must fail if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The proposed auto-split feature creates multiple sub-shipments each with a different Carrier, bypassing Supervisor approval.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy, not yet an invariant. It directly conflicts with BR-1, BR-2, and BR-3 as currently defined.","testable_assertion":"When a Shipment exceeds 500 kg, the system creates N sub-shipments each independently assigned to a Carrier without Supervisor action."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment with weight > 500 kg is created","when":"The auto-split feature triggers","then":"The original Shipment is decomposed into N sub-shipments, each with weight <= 500 kg, and each sub-shipment independently satisfies BR-1 (exactly one Carrier assigned)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment is split into sub-shipments","when":"Any sub-shipment is viewed or dispatched","then":"Each sub-shipment has exactly one Carrier, preserving the 1:1 invariant at the sub-shipment level"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A sub-shipment has been assigned a Carrier via auto-split","when":"A non-Supervisor user attempts to reassign that Carrier","then":"The reassignment is rejected, preserving the Supervisor-only reassignment rule (BR-2)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment has been auto-split into sub-shipments","when":"The system is queried for the parent Shipment's Carrier","then":"The parent Shipment's status reflects that it has been decomposed, and the original 1:1 assignment question is answered at the sub-shipment level, not the parent level"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Shipment weighing exactly 500 kg or less","when":"The auto-split feature is evaluated","then":"No split occurs; the Shipment proceeds with standard single-Carrier assignment"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Shipment weighs 501 kg but cannot be logically divided (e.g., single indivisible item)","expected_behavior":"Auto-split must be rejected or escalated; splitting is physically impossible. The system must handle indivisible freight."},{"scenario":"Carrier assignment happens before weight is finalized (weight amended after assignment)","expected_behavior":"If weight crosses 500 kg threshold after Carrier is already locked, the system faces a conflict: reassignment requires Supervisor, but auto-split wants to bypass Supervisor."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment's Carrier becomes unavailable after split","expected_behavior":"Reassignment of that sub-shipment's Carrier still requires Supervisor involvement per BR-2, unless the feature explicitly carves out an exception."},{"scenario":"Parent shipment already has a locked Carrier when auto-split triggers","expected_behavior":"Splitting requires removing/changing the parent's Carrier assignment — this is a reassignment that requires Supervisor approval under BR-2, creating a deadlock with the 'no supervisor involvement' requirement."},{"scenario":"Recursive split: a sub-shipment itself exceeds 500 kg","expected_behavior":"Must define whether sub-shipments can be recursively split or if the threshold applies only at the original Shipment level."},{"scenario":"All sub-shipments must be tracked back to the original customer order","expected_behavior":"A parent-child Shipment relationship must be introduced to maintain traceability and billing integrity."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect splitting logic could lead to billing discrepancies (who pays for each sub-shipment?), carrier contract violations, and liability gaps if accountability is unclear during transit.","operational":"Introduces a new 'sub-shipment' aggregate that does not exist today. Requires changes to dispatch, tracking, billing, and reporting. Significantly increases operational complexity.","user":"Customers expect a single shipment with a single tracking number. Splitting into multiple sub-shipments changes the customer experience and may cause confusion without proper UX design.","risk":"HIGH — this feature directly contradicts two production invariants. Shipping it without resolving the contradictions could corrupt shipment state, break dispatch guarantees, and create unauditable carrier assignments.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle a new sub-shipment concept; dispatch workflows change fundamentally for overweight shipments."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their authority over carrier reassignment is being bypassed for a specific scenario. This erodes the authorization model and may have compliance implications."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial loads they did not bid on or agree to. Carrier contracts may not support partial shipment assignments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single order may now result in multiple tracking numbers and potentially multiple delivery windows."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Must allocate costs across multiple carriers for what was a single shipment. Invoicing and reconciliation become more complex."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal","impact":"Chain-of-custody audit trail is affected. Liability for damage or loss must be clearly assigned per sub-shipment."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The feature request explicitly contradicts two established production invariants (1:1 Carrier assignment and Supervisor-only reassignment). Until the product team and domain experts resolve whether the invariants should be relaxed, scoped, or the feature redesigned, confidence in any implementation path is low.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split and BR-1/BR-2 is unambiguous and real","The edge cases identified are grounded in physical logistics reality","The 1:1 Carrier-Shipment invariant and Supervisor-only reassignment are clearly stated production rules"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to formally relax the existing invariants or is unaware of them","Whether 'sub-shipment' is meant to be a first-class domain entity or an implementation detail","How billing, tracking, and customer communication should work for split shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model as described is accurate and in production","The 500 kg threshold is a firm business rule, not an example","The product team has not yet consulted with the team that owns the Carrier assignment invariant"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning MULTIPLE Carriers to what originates as a SINGLE Shipment. This directly violates BR-1 (exactly one Carrier per Shipment). Even if sub-shipments are introduced, the act of splitting a Shipment that already has a locked Carrier constitutes a reassignment — which BR-2 says requires Supervisor approval, yet the feature explicitly requests 'without requiring supervisor involvement.'","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Supervisors","Carriers","Compliance"],  "options":["A) Introduce a formal Sub-shipment aggregate that is a first-class Shipment, each satisfying BR-1 independently. Auto-split creates new Shipments rather than modifying existing ones. Supervisor approval is still required to decommission the parent Shipment's Carrier assignment.","B) Relax BR-2 to allow system-initiated reassignment (not just Supervisor) for a specific auto-split workflow, with full audit trail. This requires explicit sign-off from domain owners.","C) Reject the feature as specified. Require that auto-split still involves Supervisor approval, making it 'assisted-split' rather than fully automatic."],"recommendation":"Option A is the safest path: model sub-shipments as independent first-class Shipments so that every invariant is preserved at the sub-shipment level. However, the transition from parent Shipment to sub-shipments still needs Supervisor sign-off (or a new domain event like 'ShipmentDecomposed') to maintain the authorization invariant. The product team must decide whether some Supervisor involvement is acceptable or whether the invariants must be formally amended."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple Carriers to a single logical Shipment","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses Supervisor approval for what constitutes Carrier reassignment","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a locked Shipment"}]}

Until the product team and domain experts resolve whether the invariants should be relaxed, scoped, or the feature redesigned, confidence in any implementation path is low.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split and BR-1/BR-2 is unambiguous and real","The edge cases identified are grounded in physical logistics reality","The 1:1 Carrier-Shipment invariant and Supervisor-only reassignment are clearly stated production rules"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to formally relax the existing invariants or is unaware of them","Whether 'sub-shipment' is meant to be a first-class domain entity or an implementation detail","How billing, tracking, and customer communication should work for split shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model as described is accurate and in production","The 500 kg threshold is a firm business rule, not an example","The product team has not yet consulted with the team that owns the Carrier assignment invariant"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning MULTIPLE Carriers to what originates as a SINGLE Shipment. This requires explicit sign-off from domain owners.","C) Reject the feature as specified.
